revisedPrompt:
Generate exactly one PNG image. Use the attached reference image as the character identity reference: match the exact same hero Mito proportions, head-to-body ratio, body width, limb thickness, palette, dark outline style, pixel density, and bright mythical-Japan traveler outfit. Treat this as re-posing the referenced character, not designing a new one.

Asset: game-development pixel-art animated sprite sheet.
Profile: snes16_platformer_v1.
Preset: hd_motion8_256.
Exact canvas size: 1024x512 pixels.
Grid layout: 4 columns x 2 rows, 8 equal cells, each cell 256x256 pixels.
Frame order: TOP row = frames 1-4 left to right, BOTTOM row = frames 5-8 left to right.
Do not draw grid lines, cell separator lines, frame borders, frame numbers, labels, text, UI, or watermark.
Background: solid #FF00FF chroma key filling all empty space, no gradients, no texture.

Character identity: original high-resolution 16-bit-plus pixel-art hero Mito, androgynous 15-year-old wanderer, lean slim action-RPG hero, about 3 heads tall, ink-black short hair with tiny low ponytail at nape, vermilion braided-cord hair ornament, calm amber eyes. Bright fantasy-Japanese traveler outfit, not samurai and not ninja: white tube-sleeved top, short indigo-blue haori jacket with small vermilion braided-cord knots on sleeves and hem, loose young-leaf-green hakama trousers tucked into dark leggings with red cord ties, light travel sandals, vermilion braided-cord belt-sash with small tassel, necklace with three pale-green magatama beads. Short ritual sword in a white scabbard at left hip, wrapped with thin sacred rope and tiny white paper streamers, reads as a festival ritual implement, not a samurai katana.

Pose/action: top-down 3/4 view, clean side profile, facing LEFT, attacking toward the left. Draw ONLY the solid body and solid short ritual sword in every frame. Sword is a straight bright blade with a white hilt wrapped with thin sacred rope and small white paper streamers fluttering slightly from motion. No motion trail, no swoosh, no afterimage, no energy effect, no glow.

Critical proportions and scale: character body about 70 percent of cell height so the fully extended sword always fits inside the cell with clear margin. Every frame must keep exactly the same head size, total standing height, body volume, body width, limb thickness, and scale. Re-pose only; do not resize, redesign, restyle, or change identity. Feet on the same ground line in every cell. Leave clear solid #FF00FF margin between the figure/sword and every cell edge.

Eight-frame sword slash to the LEFT, one continuous motion:
Frame 1: WINDUP start, facing left, grips the hilt, body coils back to the right.
Frame 2: WINDUP peak, facing left, blade raised high behind him.
Frame 3: STRIKE, blade sweeping forward in a wide arc toward the left, no trail.
Frame 4: STRIKE end, decisive key pose, full horizontal extension to the left, sword fully inside cell.
Frame 5: FOLLOW-THROUGH, swing decelerates, torso rotated, facing left.
Frame 6: FOLLOW-THROUGH settles, weight on the front foot, facing left.
Frame 7: RECOVERY, pulls blade back toward a ready guard, facing left.
Frame 8: ready guard stance facing left, loops cleanly into frame 1 for combos.

Pixel-art rendering: crisp low-resolution pixels, no antialiasing blur, high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with fine readable pixel clusters, consistent bright cheerful palette, dark one-pixel outline where useful, top-left light source. Character drawn large but contained, approximately 70% cell height. No detached fragments: no isolated pixel islands, seam slivers, dust specks, leftover effect residue, or stray marks anywhere.

Avoid all protected or unwanted likenesses and objects: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos, or likeness; no samurai armor; no kabuto helmet; no ninja outfit; no two-sword daisho; no gun; no modern objects; no horror mood; no dark gloomy palette; no bird or companion creature; no letter emblems; no modern text/watermark/UI.
